$comment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-WIDE-UNIT (FC8) end-to-end: a 64-bit-base bit-field struct (unsigned long long a:40 + b:20 packing one 64-bit unit; long long s:36 signed/sign-extending; long long pad neighbour) whose extract/insert masks EXCEED int32 (a's mask is 0xFFFFFFFFFF). Materializing a 64-bit constant > int32 was the last gap (the literal-pool dead-end) — now x86_64 emits `mov r64, imm64` (REX.W B8+rd io) and arm64 the MOVZ/MOVK ladder, capability-probed in MIR->LIR. Plus a NON-bit-field wide-const arm hi=(int)(w>>32) proving the fix beyond bit-fields. ANTI-FOLD: field sources + the wide const derive from the mutable global g_seed (loaded at runtime), so the wide masks/const are materialized at runtime in BOTH the baseline and optimized arms (a mutable global's load is opaque to ConstFold/Mem2Reg). g_seed=0xABCDEF1234: ra=0xABCDEF1234, rb=(>>12)&0xFFFFF, rs<0 (sign-extended), rpad=1000, hi=0xAB -> ok -> exit 42; any wide-mask truncation/missing-sign-extension/clobbered-neighbour/wrong-wide-const -> 7. LAYOUT-RULE-AGNOSTIC exit (write+read share gnu_packed). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
